market_summary: S&P 500 closed at 7,041.28 on April 16 — a new all-time high and the first close above 7,000 in history. Weekly gain +3.3%; Nasdaq surged +5.2% and notched its 12th consecutive positive session, the longest win streak since 2009. The Iran ceasefire (April 8) is highly fragile: violated by both sides, Iran refuses to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Trump declared a US naval blockade on April 13. Despite this instability, equity markets are pricing in full resolution. March 2026 CPI: +3.3% YoY (+0.9% MoM), a large beat driven by 21.2% monthly gasoline price surge tied to Iran war Strait of Hormuz disruption. Technology sector led with +4.82%; Energy fell -4.07% on ceasefire euphoria; Financials +2.50%. Gold (GLD) at $440.08, with XAU/USD ~$4,830/oz — actively supported by Iran war fragility and central bank inflows (920 tonnes ETF holdings, up from 840t end-2025). J.P. Morgan targets XAU $5,055 by Q4 2026.
macro_themes: 1. FED: Funds rate held at 3.50-3.75%. Next FOMC April 28-29 — critical near-term catalyst. Market consensus: two rate cuts in 2026, timing constrained by Iran- and tariff-driven inflation. Powell term expires May 2026 — succession uncertainty is a live policy risk. 2. INFLATION: March CPI +3.3% YoY, highest in months. Headline projected to peak ~4% by May-June 2026 if Hormuz reopens. Core CPI 3-month annualized ~2.9%. No rate cuts before inflation trajectory becomes clearer. 3. IRAN WAR: Fragile two-week ceasefire (April 8) mediated by Pakistan — violated by both sides. Iran refuses to reopen Strait of Hormuz. US naval blockade active since April 13. Oil supply disruption persisting; energy inflation elevated. 4. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated tariff rates (ex-China at 125%) continues. US average effective tariff rate in double digits. Business investment suppressed by policy uncertainty. 5. GOLD BULLISH CASE STRENGTHENING: XAU ~$4,830/oz. J.P. Morgan $5,055 Q4 target; RBC $4,800 year-end; Wells Fargo $6,100-6,300. Central bank inflows at multi-decade highs. Iran war fragility sustaining safe-haven premium.
portfolio_health: Grade: B- (78/100). NAV $4,323.37 — up +$97.44 (+2.30%) from April 11 baseline of $4,225.93, and +$132.37 (+3.16%) from April 4 seed of $4,191. Weekly return +2.30% vs S&P 500 +3.30% — underperformance of 1.0pp entirely attributable to 12.55% idle cash drag. The CSPX $250 buy is now in its SIXTH consecutive weekly report without execution — this is the portfolio's primary structural failure. CSPX underweight: 25.79% of total NAV vs 36% target = -10.21pp gap. Total unrealized P&L (equity positions): +$209.22 (+5.85% on cost basis ~$3,571.74). NVDA 205C call option (NVDA 17APR26 205 C) expires worthless TODAY — accept $8.50 total loss. WHT structure clean: only $3.33 withheld YTD. U03A YTM compressed to 3.68% (down from 4.3% thesis baseline). ASML China mix declining (36% to 19% of net sales) but guidance raised.

AWS AI run rate $15B, $200B 2026 capex commitment intact. Jassy tariff cost concerns on retail are manageable and do not invalidate cloud/advertising thesis. Thesis STRONGER. No action warranted — compound.

SIXTH consecutive weekly ADD recommendation. S&P 500 at new all-time high 7,041.28. CSPX at 25.79% of total portfolio NAV vs 36% target — 10.21pp gap. Buy $250 (~0.331 units at ~$756) IMMEDIATELY. Post-trade: CSPX rises to ~31.5% of NAV, cash falls to ~6.8%. Every further day of deferral while S&P sets records is a measurable compounding loss. No excuses, no further deferral.

Iran ceasefire fragile (naval blockade active April 13, Hormuz still disrupted, ceasefire violated by both sides). Central bank ETF inflows to 920 tonnes. XAU/USD ~$4,830/oz. J.P. Some analyst flags on volume deceleration and regulatory scrutiny merit attention at Q1 print. Remains Tranche 3 co-target ($100 add) on S&P 5-7% pullback or post-earnings confirmation. Thesis SAME pending April 30 earnings.

$1 trillion in confirmed purchase orders through 2027 announced at GTC 2026 (purchase orders, not projections). Up +18% in 10 consecutive sessions. At $198.35, 9.0% above $181.98 cost basis. Vera Rubin platform and 2028 Feynman roadmap confirmed innovation pipeline. Do not add after 18% surge — let compound. Next earnings May 20. Thesis STRONGER.

NVDA 205 Call expires today (April 17, 2026). NVDA at $198.35 — OTM by $6.65. Expires worthless. Total loss: $9.57 original premium. No roll warranted. Accept defined-risk loss. Position closes at zero today.

YTM compressed to 3.68% (from 4.3% thesis baseline) — 62bps yield decline. WHT-free structure remains optimal for Lebanese resident via Irish fund portfolio interest exemption. Opportunity cost vs S&P at ATH rising. Plan staged reduction from ~14% toward 10% of NAV as: (1) CSPX gap closes, (2) Tranche 3 deploys, (3) Fed rate cut signals emerge from April 28-29 FOMC. No immediate trim today. Thesis WEAKER.

rebalancing: Urgently warranted on two fronts. (1) CSPX ADD $250 — sixth consecutive recommendation; post-trade closes CSPX gap from -10.21pp to -4.5pp, reduces cash drag from 12.55% to 6.8%. (2) Tranche 3 ($242.41 post-CSPX) must deploy by May 3 hard deadline (16 days): deploy $200 CSPX unconditionally if no 5-7% S&P pullback; or $142.41 CSPX + $100 MA on pullback. Medium-term: U03A trim from ~14% toward 10% of NAV as Fed rate cuts materialize. No other positions require rebalancing.

STEP 1 IMMEDIATE: Deploy $250 into CSPX — cash falls to ~$292.41 (~6.8% of post-trade NAV). STEP 2 TRANCHE 3 (May 3 hard deadline, 16 days): Trigger A — S&P drops 5-7% to ~6,548-6,609: deploy $142.41 CSPX + $100 MA. Trigger B — May 3 hard deadline: deploy $200 CSPX unconditionally, retain ~$42.41 minimum buffer. Cash must not remain above 10% of NAV past May 3.

new_opportunities.rationale: Broadcom: highest-conviction new-position candidate once NAV exceeds $5,000. AI custom silicon leadership (Google TPU, Meta AI accelerator), 24% total revenue growth, 74% AI semiconductor revenue growth, VMware networking integration. Minimal dividend (~1.3%) — WHT impact ~$3-4/year at suggested size, acceptable. Complements NVDA as second AI semiconductor name. DO NOT BUY NOW — close CSPX gap and deploy Tranche 3 first. Revisit after May 3 Tranche 3 deployment with NAV > $5,000.

Meta Platforms: zero dividend = 0% WHT. $60B 2026 AI infrastructure capex, Llama open-source ecosystem moat, advertising duopoly with Alphabet, near-term AI monetization more visible than most peers. Research candidate — not actionable until NAV > $5,000 and CSPX gap fully closed.
